MB search returned no release/candidates, so no tracklist to verify keyTracks for our album; the keyTracks list looks mixed ('Levees' and 'The Water' are A Tale of God's Will tracks per the wiki; 'Congo Square'/'Ghost of Congo Square' may belong to the actual Congo Square album). Repair writer must source genuine Congo Square (2004/2005, Blue Note) content.

wrongClaims.ourClaim: Orobas: Book of Angels Volume 4 is an album by Koby Israelite performing compositions from John Zorn's second Masada book, "The Book of Angels".
wrongClaims.problem: The item is 'Malphas: Book of Angels, Volume 3' (performed by Mark Feldman & Sylvie Courvoisier per MB), but the albumDNA describes a DIFFERENT volume, 'Orobas: Book of Angels Volume 4' by Koby Israelite. Wrong album in the series.
wrongClaims.evidenceQuote: Orobas: Book of Angels Volume 4 is an album by Koby Israelite performing compositions from John Zorn's second Masada book, "The Book of Angels".

notes: MB embedded_mbid confirms OUR album is Malphas Vol 3 (Tzadik, 2006-01-24, performers Mark Feldman & Sylvie Courvoisier; Zorn is composer). But the albumDNA and the stored_url are both about Orobas Vol 4 (Koby Israelite) - a different release. keyTracks Azriel/Basus/Rigal/Kafziel match MB; 'Labariel' does not match (MB has 'Lahariel' - almost certainly a one-letter typo). Note: our artist field 'John Zorn' is the composer, not the performing artist MB lists, but that is conventional for the Book of Angels series and not the basis for this verdict; the verdict is driven by the swapped prose.

wrongClaims.ourClaim: Jelly Roll Morton: The Complete Library of Congress Recordings is a 2005 box set of recordings from jazz pioneer Jelly Roll Morton. The set spans 128 tracks over eight CDs. It won two Grammy Awards in 2006, Best Historical Album and Best Album Notes.
wrongClaims.problem: Our album is the single-disc 'The Library of Congress Recordings by Alan Lomax' (Rounder, 2006 per MB), but the albumDNA describes the 8-CD/128-track box set 'The Complete Library of Congress Recordings' (2005) and assigns that box set's Grammys. Same artist and source recordings, but a different release.
wrongClaims.evidenceQuote: In 2007, Rounder released Jelly Roll Morton: The Library of Congress Recordings by Alan Lomax, a single disc consisting of selected highlights from the box set.

notes: Borderline but graded entity_mismatch: the prose's hard facts (8 CDs, 128 tracks, 2005, two 2006 Grammys) belong to the COMPLETE box set, a distinct release (distinct MBID/wiki page) from our single-disc highlights album. MB (search, similarity 1.0) confirms OUR release as 'The Library of Congress Recordings by Alan Lomax' (Rounder), and all 5 keyTracks match its tracklist. The stored_url points to the box set's page, not our single-disc release (hence wrong_entity). Repair writer: keep the Jelly Roll Morton/Library of Congress identity, but rewrite to describe the single-disc highlights release, not the box set.

Right entity: MB embedded_mbid resolves to a Billie Holiday compilation titled 'The Man I Love' whose 22-track listing matches our keyTracks exactly (incl. the shared typos 'As Yime Goes By', 'You Can't Loose a Broken Heart'), and the albumDNA's named tracks ('God Bless the Child', 'Fine and Mellow') are both on it. Per the spec, the embedded MBID is authoritative for date and label, and it gives date 2002-04-09 and label Dreyfus Jazz, which contradict our year 2007 and label 'AVM Music' -> factual_error. (Plausibly a 2007 AVM reissue of the same comp, but the resolved release does not support those values; flag for repair.) The stored wiki link 'The Man I Love (album)' resolves to a 1957 PEGGY LEE album - wrong entity - so it is discarded for fact-checking and recorded as wrong_entity; the albumDNA is NOT drawn from that Peggy Lee page.

That candidate entity-matches our album (title + the ensemble led by our artist Darcy James Argue), so the identity is very likely right, but with no resolved release there is no tracklist to verify keyTracks (Phobos/Habeas Corpus/Transit/Zeno) -> keyTracksVerdict no_evidence, and no label/date detail to check. No contradictions, but nothing substantive is verifiable against confident evidence -> unverifiable. No wiki stored/found.
